text:
Once upon an autumn dusk, in a quiet village overlooked by ancient oaks, there lived a girl named Alice.

Every evening, Alice listened to the wind whisper through the trees, telling tales of old. Most nights she'd listen to stories of brave squirrels. One particular squirrel had an obsession with a shiny red feather. Alice knew every boast the squirrel made.

One day, the feather vanished. Panic struck. Through days turned into weeks, Alice didn't speak much.

Then night fell deep, velvet-black.

The wise owl hooted gently.

"It was the red feather," the owl croaked.

Alice blinked. "You knew."

"How?” said the owl. “Maybe the squirrels?"

"I think you gave it back."

The owl perched. "Where?"

"To you."

A silence. Owls like that?

"I know," Alice whispered.

Days crawled. Midnight came.

There.

The squirrels had woven it into a knot.

Alice watched as they worked. After hours,
